# CANDIDATE: TRENDLINE_ONLY_MORNING_GATE

**Filed:** 2026-06-18  
**Filer:** chef-nemotron (free-tier autonomous R&D)  
**Type:** filter_change  
**Status:** DRAFT (NEEDS-RATIFICATION per Rule 9)

## Hypothesis

Blocking single-trigger trendline_rejection entries during the morning session (09:35-11:30 ET) reduces losses on choppy, low-volume mornings while preserving higher-conviction afternoon trend entries. Morning trendline-only setups often fail due to incomplete structure (missing confluence/ribbon flip) and are prone to stop-outs before the trend develops.

## Mechanism

- Entry condition: trendline_rejection_bearish trigger fires as a single trigger (no confluence, no ribbon_flip, no level_reclaim)  
- Time window: 09:35 ET to 11:30 ET  
- Action: BLOCK the entry (return None from evaluate_bearish_setup)  
- All other entry types (multi-trigger, confluence, level_reclaim, ribbon_flip) remain unaffected  
- Exit logic unchanged: follows BEARISH_REJECTION_RIDE_THE_RIBBON rules (TP1 at +30%, runner to BE after TP1, chart stop at rejected level +$0.50 buffer, time stop 15:50 ET)

## OP-20 disclosures

1. **Account-size assumption:** qty=28 requires $25K+ account; $1K paper account ~= 14% headline P&L (per risk-rules.md position sizing table).  
2. **Sample bias:** Stage-1 backtest would use 16-month SPY 5m data (2025-01-01 to 2026-06-16). Selection method: all trendline_rejection_bearish triggers filtered to single-trigger only in 09:35-11:30 ET window. Overfit risk: high if gate parameters (time window) are optimized to J anchor days without OOS validation.  
3. **Out-of-sample:** NEEDS-OOS (walk-forward test not yet performed; requires IS/OOS split with ratio>=0.70 gate).  
4. **Real-fills:** NEEDS-REAL-FILLS (top 3 J days not yet validated with realistic OPRA simulation; requires bid-ask/slippage model).  
5. **Failure modes:**  
   - Worst day: 5/04 (J winner) if blocked entry causes missed opportunity (delta = -$730)  
   - Max drawdown: potential -$1,000+ if multiple morning winners blocked in volatile regime  
   - Blow-up scenario: persistent afternoon trend regime where morning block filters out early entry signals, reducing runner profitability  
6. **Concentration:** unknown -- requires Stage-1 backtest (if top-5 days = X% of P&L, state X; currently unavailable).

## Pre-merge gate

<what tests need to pass: gym validators, walk-forward, real-fills>  
- Gym v41 validators: 81/81 PASS (no new state variables)  
- Walk-forward OOS test: IS/OOS split (e.g., 2025-01-01..2025-06-30 IS, 2025-07-01..2026-06-16 OOS) with OOS/IS Sharpe ratio >= 0.70  
- Real-fills validation: top 3 J days (4/29, 5/01, 5/04) must show <±20% diff between BS-sim and realistic OPRA fills (cached real bids/asks)  
- Anchor regression test: edge_capture on J days must not decrease by >$50 vs baseline (preserve 4/29 +53.6/c, 5/01 +$470, 5/04 +$730)  
- Concentration check: top-5 days <= 60% of total P&L (avoid over-reliance on few days)

## Confidence

3 / 10 -- No backtest data available; hypothesis based on morning chop observation from leaderboard notes (e.g., 5/05 loser day morphology). High risk of overfitting to morning loser days without OOS validation.

## Pre-existing leaderboard impact

Complements rank 21 (MIDDAY_TRENDLINE_GATE): morning gate (09:35-11:30) + midday gate (11:30-14:00) create contiguous blocked window for low-quality trendline entries. Does not conflict with any existing candidates; may synergize with V14E_BEAR_TIME_OF_DAY_GATE (rank 17) which elevates threshold during 10:xx-11:xx chop zone. No duplication of effort: morning gate targets single-trigger trendline only, while V14E gate targets v14e bear observations. Combined, they address different failure modes in the morning session.
